title: Deletion propagation check
description: Check deletion across primary records, derived tables, caches, indexes, queues, analytics, exports, and external processors.
body: Use when changing account deletion, retention cleanup, schema storage, search indexing, or derived features. Do not claim deletion for backups or processors without project evidence. Steps: identify all copies, run local deletion or expiry path, verify derived records are removed or disassociated, and document systems that require manual or asynchronous handling. Validation signals include targeted tests and before/after store checks. Risk boundary: orphaned regulated data or external copies require human gate. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
